module_mood_contract: Joyful creation — 'it works, on my repo.' Student discovers the compound loop on a real bug from their own backlog; the artifact (CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence) is unambiguously theirs. Any edit that makes M1 feel like a TDD tutorial or a tools-setup module steals the personal-authorship feeling that sets up M2's grounded-competence deepening.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands at 8+ per phase and close.
Mood doesn't resolve the next module's teaching moment early.
Named teaching moment reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Compound step (Ex3: CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence) is forced inside the exercise structure. The module's Debrief block names the move; the exercise file carries the prompt. Student cannot complete Ex3 without running the retro. Push-back moves for compound rubber-stamping are enumerated in the maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea honors the strategy doc's M1 mood contract and framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea matches strategy doc M1 section precisely: three-phase loop (orient+introspect, fix-tests-first, compound-and-close), same artifacts (./CLAUDE.local.md, PR, ticket closed via connector). Joyful creation mood contract honored throughout module body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: This file's close hands off to M2's open hook.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:48 — 'Module 2 is where plan mode earns its keep: multi-file work, a second pass that pressure-tests the plan...' Matches M2's grounded-competence mood. ./CLAUDE.local.md at the top of the next session is explicitly named as the M2 context-carry artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers. Demo lectures (ceiling-showing) go at the beginning; conceptual vocabulary lectures land mid-module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:23 — '[Painting the picture with the LLM](lectures/painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md)' is placed as the FIRST lecture in the module flow — BEFORE [The wizard move] and BEFORE all three exercises. The lecture's own maintainer block (curriculum/lectures/painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md line 38) explicitly states: '~3 min closer for Module 1, after the compound-and-close exercise. Role: closer.' The lecture names the mirror theme ('your stance is the ceiling') — a pattern the student lives through M1. Placing it before the exercises means the student hears the conclusion before the felt evidence. This steals the joyful-creation close moment. The module's original sequence ([The wizard move] opener + exercises + [How this training was built] closer) was correct; [Painting the picture with the LLM] should replace or follow [How this training was built] as a second closer, per its own maintainer-declared role.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move [Painting the picture with the LLM] to closer position — after the Debrief, at or near [How this training was built]. The lecture's own maintainer block names it a closer; the module placement contradicts that declaration.

rules_evaluated.evidence: M1 is joyful creation, not a contrast-mood module (rule 2 targets M5-style contrast-mood arc). Even so, with the [Painting the picture with the LLM] misplacement, two lectures now precede the exercises (lines 23-25). If the misplacement is fixed (lecture moved to closer), one demo lecture (wizard move) remains before exercises — correct dosage regardless.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:21 — 'The room harvests everyone's before the training adds its own.' Covered by the Connections room-read carve-out (check_pedagogy.md rule 27 + compounded/2026-05-03-pedagogy-connections-room-read-carveout.md). The maintainer block documents the exception at lines 65-66. Bare harvest sentence as its own paragraph after the Connections question = within carve-out. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module file contains no fenced prompt blocks. All forcing functions live in the referenced exercise files. The module-level body describes the arc without embedding prose forcing functions. Architectural split is clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block contains explicit leap test section with three named, falsifiable outcomes: (1) runs /context on a working session without prompting — falsifiable by scrollback; (2) writes a failing test before touching the fix — falsifiable by commit history or diff; (3) edits ./CLAUDE.local.md from session evidence with a rule quoting a specific session beat — falsifiable by the rule content. All three use named verbs and artifacts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function documents its dominant failure and one recovery move.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block contains explicit per-phase failure mode + escape hatch table covering all eight forcing functions (Connections trick, Ex1 introspection, Ex1 /context, Ex2 tests-first, Ex2 diff review, Ex3 compound, Ex3 team-worthy flag, Ex3 quoted-evidence, Ex3 MCP gate). Each row names failure mode and escape hatch. Format matches rule 47.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honored — does not flatten or pre-resolve.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block: 'Mood target: joyful creation — it works, on my repo.' Module body stays in joyful-creation register. Bridge teases M2's plan-mode discipline without delivering it. Contract honored. Note: the misplaced closing lecture (Painting the picture with the LLM at opener position) slightly threatens the mood arc — the lecture names the mirror theme before the student experiences it — but the module's exercises still deliver the felt evidence. The REVISE on check_lectures.md rule 1 is the load-bearing fix; mood score survives at 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets all lead with positive disciplines: loop shape, /context as tool, self-report as hypothesis, failing test as proof, CLAUDE.local.md as compound artifact, ticket close as first-move-past-repo. No failure-word titles.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — draft doesn't front-run a downstream teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No Skill use at M1 per strategy doc. Module correctly uses /context (slash command) and MCP (connector). Three-block memory seeded without naming. Plan mode deferred to M2. Klaassen attribution inside Ex3 retro. No downstream moments front-run. Key Concepts bullet 1 references 'M1' in student-facing body prose — this is a cross-module forward-reference ('every later module rides on this shape') but matches the strategy doc's explicit M1 framing and is not a spoiler of a downstream teaching moment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts — each bullet surfaces a theme instance.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets surface: compounding (loop shape + CLAUDE.local.md compound), self-awareness (90% misrepresentation + self-report as hypothesis), capture-your-world (rules grown from evidence, not blank pages), non-agentic-default (ticket close as first-move-past-repo — the loop ending outside the code is the anti-non-agentic-default beat). Two to three themes surfaced per rule 5.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — run §§3/4/5 gates BEFORE decorating with breadcrumbs; breadcrumbs on survivors only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Gates §3 (discipline-first), §4 (no front-running), §5 (themes surface) all PASS. Survivors decorated: 3 of 6 bullets carry **Remember:** breadcrumbs. Non-breadcrumbed bullets remain as tactical module voice. Breadcrumb phrasing uses engineer register: 'context is what you put in it' / 'assume 90% on first pass' / 'rules grow from evidence, not blank pages'. No standalone **Themes this module instances:** section. Shape clean.
